stemHtml:
An aircraft fitted with a basic version of EGPWS is cruising at FL80 in clear skies. The terrain along this part of the route has a maximum elevation of 1000 ft. Suddenly, the caution ‘TOO LOW TERRAIN’ sounds unexpectedly. The pilot looks out and the distance to the terrain below remains as expected for the altitude compared to the elevation. Given the information below.

Attitude indicator: level flight
Radio altimeter: setting 1013 hPa, reading 400 ft

What is the most probable cause for this caution to sound?

options.a: The radio altimeter is faulty, giving an incorrect indication that triggers the caution.
options.b: The altimeter is set to the wrong pressure, giving inaccurate altitude indications and triggering the caution.
options.c: Rapid changes in elevation may cause an oversensitive EGPWS to issue a spurious caution.
options.d: The aeroplane has an excessively high closure rate towards higher terrain ahead.
